sections.paragraph:
ἀναντίρρητος, ον (also ἀναντίρητος; W-S. §5, 266b) pass. (ῥητός ‘spoken’; Polyb. 6, 7, 7; Diod S 31, 27, 7; Plut., Them. 124a [24, 4], Mor. 339b; Job 11:2 Sym.) not to be contradicted, undeniable (Herm. Wr. 2, 11 ἀ. ὁ λόγος; Jos., C. Ap. 1, 160) ἀ. ὄντων τούτων since this is undeniable Ac 19:36. βραβεῖον ἀ. ἀποφέρεσθαι carry off an incontestable prize MPol 17:1.—DELG s.v. εἴρω.

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 68
